name: Nine Gods of Creation
description: Also known as ✨ The Ennead (Nine Gods of Heliopolis).
extracted_title: The Ennead - Nine Gods of Creation

keyMyths.title: Atum's Self-Creation
keyMyths.description: The central myth recounts Atum's emergence from the primordial waters of Nun. He created himself through thought and will, standing on the primordial mound, Benben, and initiating the process of creation.

keyMyths.title: The Birth of Shu and Tefnut
keyMyths.description: Atum created Shu (air) and Tefnut (moisture) through either masturbation or spitting, depending on the version of the myth. Their births represented the first act of differentiation and the beginning of order in the cosmos.

keyMyths.title: The Separation of Nut and Geb
keyMyths.description: Shu, the god of air, separated his children Nut (sky) and Geb (earth), who were initially locked in an embrace. This separation created the space for life and the world as we know it, preventing chaos from returning.

keyMyths.title: The Birth of Osiris, Isis, Seth, and Nephthys
keyMyths.description: Nut, against the wishes of Ra, secretly gave birth to Osiris, Isis, Seth, and Nephthys. These deities represent key aspects of Egyptian society, including kingship, magic, chaos, and protection.

keyMyths.title: The Conflict Between Osiris and Seth
keyMyths.description: The rivalry between Osiris and Seth, culminating in Osiris's murder, is a central theme. This conflict highlights the struggle between order and chaos and the eventual triumph of order through Horus, Osiris's son.

sources.name: Pyramid Texts
sources.description: The oldest collection of Egyptian religious texts, inscribed on the walls of pyramids from the Old Kingdom.
sources.title: Pyramid Texts

sources.name: Coffin Texts
sources.description: A collection of funerary spells and texts inscribed on coffins during the Middle Kingdom.
sources.title: Coffin Texts

sources.name: Book of the Dead
sources.description: A collection of spells and texts intended to guide the deceased through the afterlife, commonly written on papyrus scrolls.
sources.title: Book of the Dead

sources.name: Papyrus Jumilhac
sources.description: A late Ptolemaic papyrus containing religious and mythological texts, including stories about the Ennead.
sources.title: Papyrus Jumilhac

sources.name: Plutarch, *De Iside et Osiride*
sources.description: A Greek philosophical treatise that provides a detailed account of the myth of Osiris and Isis.
sources.title: Plutarch, *De Iside et Osiride*

deities.name: Goddess of Moisture & Dew | Egyptian Mythology
